root_text.an7.73:1.1: “Bhūtapubbaṁ, bhikkhave, sunetto nāma satthā ahosi titthakaro kāmesu vītarāgo.
root_text.an7.73:1.2: Sunettassa kho pana, bhikkhave, satthuno anekāni sāvakasatāni ahesuṁ.
root_text.an7.73:1.3: Sunetto satthā sāvakānaṁ brahmalokasahabyatāya dhammaṁ desesi.
root_text.an7.73:1.4: Ye kho pana, bhikkhave, sunettassa satthuno brahmalokasahabyatāya dhammaṁ desentassa cittāni nappasādesuṁ te kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā apāyaṁ duggatiṁ vinipātaṁ nirayaṁ upapajjiṁsu.
root_text.an7.73:1.5: Ye kho pana, bhikkhave, sunettassa satthuno brahmalokasahabyatāya dhammaṁ desentassa cittāni pasādesuṁ te kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjiṁsu.
root_text.an7.73:2.1: Bhūtapubbaṁ, bhikkhave, mūgapakkho nāma satthā ahosi …pe…
root_text.an7.73:2.6: arako nāma satthā ahosi titthakaro kāmesu vītarāgo.
root_text.an7.73:2.7: Arakassa kho pana, bhikkhave, satthuno anekāni sāvakasatāni ahesuṁ.
root_text.an7.73:2.8: Arako nāma satthā sāvakānaṁ brahmalokasahabyatāya dhammaṁ desesi.
root_text.an7.73:2.9: Ye kho pana, bhikkhave, arakassa satthuno brahmalokasahabyatāya dhammaṁ desentassa cittāni nappasādesuṁ, te kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā apāyaṁ duggatiṁ vinipātaṁ nirayaṁ upapajjiṁsu.
root_text.an7.73:2.10: Ye kho pana, bhikkhave, arakassa satthuno brahmalokasahabyatāya dhammaṁ desentassa cittāni pasādesuṁ, te kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjiṁsu.
root_text.an7.73:3.2: yo ime satta satthāre titthakare kāmesu vītarāge anekasataparivāre sasāvakasaṅghe paduṭṭhacitto akkoseyya paribhāseyya, bahuṁ so apuññaṁ pasaveyyā”ti?
root_text.an7.73:3.4: “Yo, bhikkhave, ime satta satthāre titthakare kāmesu vītarāge anekasataparivāre sasāvakasaṅghe paduṭṭhacitto akkoseyya paribhāseyya, bahuṁ so apuññaṁ pasaveyya.
root_text.an7.73:3.5: Yo ekaṁ diṭṭhisampannaṁ puggalaṁ paduṭṭhacitto akkosati paribhāsati, ayaṁ tato bahutaraṁ apuññaṁ pasavati.
root_text.an7.73:3.7: Nāhaṁ, bhikkhave, ito bahiddhā evarūpiṁ khantiṁ vadāmi yathāmaṁ sabrahmacārīsu.

translation_text.an7.73:1.1: “Once upon a time, mendicants, there was a Teacher named Sunetta. He was a religious founder and was free of desire for sensual pleasures.
translation_text.an7.73:1.2: He had many hundreds of disciples.
translation_text.an7.73:1.3: He taught them the path to rebirth in the company of Divinity.
translation_text.an7.73:1.4: Those lacking confidence in Sunetta were—when their body broke up, after death—reborn in a place of loss, a bad place, the underworld, hell.
translation_text.an7.73:1.5: Those full of confidence in Sunetta were—when their body broke up, after death—reborn in a good place, a heavenly realm.
translation_text.an7.73:2.1: Once upon a time there was a teacher named Mūgapakkha …
translation_text.an7.73:2.6: Araka. He was a religious founder and was free of desire for sensual pleasures.
translation_text.an7.73:2.7: He had many hundreds of disciples.
translation_text.an7.73:2.8: He taught them the way to rebirth in the company of Divinity.
translation_text.an7.73:2.9: Those lacking confidence in Araka were—when their body broke up, after death—reborn in a place of loss, a bad place, the underworld, hell.
translation_text.an7.73:2.10: Those full of confidence in Araka were—when their body broke up, after death—reborn in a good place, a heavenly realm.
translation_text.an7.73:3.2: If someone with malicious intent were to abuse and insult these seven teachers with their hundreds of followers, would they not brim with much wickedness?”
translation_text.an7.73:3.5: But someone who abuses and insults a single individual accomplished in view with malicious intent brims even more with wickedness.
translation_text.an7.73:3.7: I say that any injury done by those outside of the Buddhist community does not compare with what is done to one’s own spiritual companions.
translation_text.an7.73:4.1: So you should train like this:
translation_text.an7.73:4.2: ‘We will have no malicious intent for our spiritual companions.’

variant_text.an7.73:1.4: kho pana, bhikkhave → ye kho bhikkhave (bj, sya-all)
variant_text.an7.73:4.2: na no sabrahmacārīsu → na tveva amhaṁ sabrahmacārīsu (bj, sya-all)
